title: Farmhouse Indoor Playhouse
summary: A complete standalone gable-roofed playhouse built from 1x2 furring-strip frames skinned in thin plywood, with a working door opening, two windows with boxes, and an eight-panel gable roof.
description:
This is a full four-wall playhouse, built as five separate wall and roof assemblies rather than one continuous structure — the two gable (side) walls, the front and back walls, and the roof each get their own 1x2 furring-strip frame skinned with 1/4" plywood, so the whole thing can be built flat on a floor before standing up. **Cut every plywood panel from full sheets in the first step**, since the gable walls, front/back walls, and eight roof panels are all sized to nest efficiently across just two sheets.

The front wall carries the working door opening — frame the door sides, top, and bottom plate carefully, since this is the one opening in the whole structure that needs to function smoothly for repeated use. The roof assembly centers on a single 2x2 ridge beam with 1x2 eaves running the length on both sides, and the roof panels attach to the frame with self-tapping screws once the walls are standing and square.

Two windows, each framed with its own small box, add real farmhouse character — build the window boxes as separate small frames and mount them to the finished walls last, once the main structure is solid and true.

tools.note: For cutting the door opening and window openings.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Longest point measurement, one end cut at 45°.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at 45°.
Cut first so you don't have to hold onto tiny pieces while cutting.

steps.title: Cut the plywood panels
steps.body: Cut the gable, front/back wall panels, and eight roof panels from the plywood sheets first, since they nest efficiently across the two sheets.

steps.title: Build the gable wall frames
steps.body: Build the two gable wall frames from the rafters, corners, and horizontal trim, then skin with plywood.

steps.title: Build the back wall
steps.body: Build the back wall frame from its top/bottom plate and corners, then skin with plywood.

steps.title: Build the front wall with door opening
steps.body: Build the front wall frame with its top plate, corners, door sides and top, and bottom plates, cutting the working door opening with a jigsaw, then skin with plywood.

steps.title: Assemble the walls
steps.body: Stand and join the four walls together with self-tapping screws.

steps.title: Build and attach the roof
steps.body: Assemble the ridge beam and eaves, then attach the eight roof panels to the frame.

steps.title: Add the windows and boxes
steps.body: Cut the window openings with a jigsaw, frame them, and mount the small window boxes to the finished walls.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Farmhouse style indoor playhouse with gable roof
